content: Laravel authorization bypass pitfalls: (1) Gate::define() in AuthServiceProvider but forgetting to call Gate::policy() or register in $policies array — policy is defined but never used. (2) Using can() middleware on routes but controller methods do not call $this->authorize() — middleware passes on gate name mismatch while the actual controller action is unprotected. (3) Route model binding can bypass authorization if policy methods use wrong model type hint. (4) Artisan commands (queue workers, scheduled tasks) bypass HTTP middleware entirely — any authorization defined only in middleware is invisible to queue jobs. In TenantSmith, module installation via InstallTenantModule job runs outside HTTP context so admin checks must be in the service layer too.
